title: Attributes, Filters, Merge Drivers, and Content Policy · History Workshop
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first path-attribute and content-transformation policy for attributes, filters, merge drivers, and content policy. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of path-attribute and content-transformation policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.
Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.
Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G35 is a workshop in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-large-content-policy-1prqsn" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Large Content Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-large-content-policy-1prqsn
# scenario-key: trace-1prqsn probe-nsqrp1 boundary-rqsn1p owner-1prqsnx fault-1prqsnz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1p candidate=b00b1sn
# hypothesis: a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy
git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh
# expected: the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path
# observation: release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy
# changed-case: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data
# after-ref: main=a11ce1p candidate=c0ffeen
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref
# transfer-limit: test real filters and encodings across supported platforms in disposable clones
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref. Competency probe: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Any large file automatically belongs in a pointer-based extension regardless of access and history needs.

stimulus.title: Gita Large Content Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.

options.text: Keep the plausible Gita Large Content Policy output and hide the violated path-attribute and content-transformation policy assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Large Content Policy without its stated preconditions, omit the path-attribute and content-transformation policy trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Large Content Policy for path-attribute and content-transformation policy.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Large Content Policy condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.why: Retrieving Gita Large Content Policy through the concrete path-attribute and content-transformation policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.
